apps.name: Excalidraw
apps.description: A virtual whiteboard for sketching hand-drawn diagrams. Fully offline-capable, collaborative, and open source.

apps.name: Photopea
apps.description: A full-featured photo editor in the browser. Opens PSD, XCF, Sketch and RAW files — a serious Photoshop alternative.

apps.name: Squoosh
apps.description: Compress and compare images with different codecs, right in your browser. Built by the Chrome team as a PWA showcase.

apps.name: SVGOMG
apps.description: The missing GUI for SVGO — optimize SVG files with live preview. A tiny, perfect example of what a PWA can be.

apps.name: VS Code for the Web
apps.description: Visual Studio Code running entirely in your browser. Open local folders with the File System Access API — no install needed.

apps.name: Monkeytype
apps.description: A minimalist, customizable typing test. Track your speed and accuracy with beautiful themes and detailed stats.

apps.name: Pinterest
apps.description: Visual discovery engine for ideas. One of the most cited PWA success stories — engagement rose 60% after their PWA rebuild.

apps.name: X (Twitter)
apps.description: The X social network as a lightweight installable app — push notifications, offline timelines, and a fraction of the native app's size.

apps.name: Spotify Web Player
apps.description: Stream Spotify's full catalog from an installable web player with media-key and lock-screen controls.

apps.name: Telegram Web
apps.description: Fast, installable Telegram client with push notifications and near feature-parity with the native apps.

apps.name: Starbucks
apps.description: Order ahead and browse the menu even on flaky connections. The PWA is 99.84% smaller than the iOS app and doubled daily active users.

apps.name: Uber
apps.description: Request a ride from a 50KB core app that loads in under 3 seconds on 2G networks — built for riders on any device, anywhere.
